key_insight: Atmospheric plasma-sprayed metal–graphite composite coatings can improve both corrosion resistance and electrical contact performance for titanium PEMFC bipolar plates, with higher graphite content giving better results.
engineering_relevance: The work points to a practical coating route for making lightweight metal bipolar plates more durable and conductive, supporting PEM fuel cell commercialization.
method: APS deposited Ni–graphite and Cu–graphite coatings on titanium bipolar plates
Varied graphite content to study its effect on phase composition and microstructure
Evaluated corrosion behavior and interfacial contact resistance before and after cathodic polarization
evidence_signal: The best reported coatings reached potentiostatic corrosion current densities of 1.05 × 10^-4 A·cm^-2 (Ni–G) and 0.91 × 10^-4 A·cm^-2 (Cu–G), with ICR values as low as 25.2 and 17.4 mΩ·cm^2 before cathodic polarization.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on graphite loading and coating integrity, so long-term durability, adhesion, and scalable spray repeatability still need validation.
paper_title: Investigation of Metal-Graphite Coatings for PEMFC Bipolar Plate Using Atmospheric Plasma Thermal Spraying
